* fix(deploy): link script to GCP project before calling scripts.run

The Execution API returns HTTP 403 when the Apps Script project is
associated with a different GCP project than the OAuth credentials.
After project creation, PUT /v1/projects/{scriptId} with the user's
GCP project number to establish the required link before scripts.run
is called.

- Add GCP Project Number input to Step 1 UI
- Read parentId from input and call PUT /projects/{scriptId} after creation
- Guard handleDeploy and setDeployEnabled against missing GCP number
- Update mockSuccessfulDeploy and all individual test route handlers
  to intercept the new PUT /projects/{id} call
- Add tests: disabled without GCP number, re-disables on clear,
  handleDeploy guard, parentId sent correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(deploy): remove scripts.run — Execution API incompatible with browser flow

The Apps Script Execution API (scripts.run) requires the script project
to be associated with the same GCP project as the OAuth credentials.
Scripts created via REST API always get Google's default managed GCP
project, and there is no REST endpoint to change this association — it
requires manual action in the Apps Script editor.

The previous GCP Project Number input and PUT /projects/{scriptId} fix
also did not work: parentId in projects.create/update is a Drive file ID
(for container-bound scripts), not a GCP project number.

Remove scripts.run and all related infrastructure:
- Drop scripts.run calls and the version/deployment fallback
- Remove GCP Project Number input field (wrong problem)
- Remove sleep() helper (only used for deployment propagation delay)
- Remove version and deployment creation steps
- Simplify OAUTH_SCOPE to script.projects only
- Remove executionApi and oauthScopes from APPSSCRIPT_MANIFEST
- Always show "run setup() once from the editor" trigger instruction
- Update setup instructions to list only the required scope

Update tests:
- Restore signIn() to Client ID only (no GCP number)
- Remove PUT /projects (non-content) route handlers
- Remove :run, /versions, /deployments route handlers
- Remove GCP number guard and parentId tests
- Replace conditional trigger note tests with fixed manual instruction test
- Update manifest assertions to match simplified manifest

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove project name input, always use Petry-Projects prefix

The project title was derived differently for single vs multi-script
deploys — single used the user's raw input ('My Google Apps Script')
while multi appended the script name ('My Google Apps Script – Gmail…').
This meant the localStorage idempotency key differed between the two
flows, causing a new project to be created on each redeploy instead of
reusing the existing one.

Fix: remove the project name input entirely and always derive the title
as 'Petry-Projects – {script.name}'. This is deterministic regardless
of how many scripts are selected, so the idempotency key is stable
across all deploys of the same script.

- Remove Step 4 'Name your Apps Script project' card and input
- setDeployEnabled no longer requires a title — only token + script
- handleDeploy derives projectTitle as 'Petry-Projects – {script.name}'
- Remove title input event listener
- Update Playwright tests: remove title-input tests, update idempotency
  storage keys and captured-title assertions to match new naming

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Support Marshal. (#125)

Phase 1 - Ruby language feature completion:
- Add Hash#transform_keys/values, #slice, #except, #dig, #each_with_object
- Add Enumerable#filter_map, #tally, #each_with_object, #chunk, #chunk_while, #flat_map, #sum, #zip, #uniq

- Add Array#sum, #tally, #filter_map, #union, #intersection, #difference
- Add NotImplementedError, EncodingError, IOError, EOFError, Errno::* classes

Phase 2 - Standard library implementation:
- Add Set class (pure Ruby, Hash-backed)
- Add Date/DateTime classes with chrono-compatible interface
- Add StringIO class
- Add JSON module (parse/generate/dump)
- Improve Monitor/MonitorMixin
- Improve StringScanner

* Add JSON common module for stdlib implementation

Work around monoruby limitations:
- No String#capitalize: use manual [0].upcase + [1..-1].downcase
- No Integer#abs: add polyfill
- No public_send: use send instead
- No define_singleton_method: use singleton_class.define_method
- No self.extended hook: rewrite Concern
- HashWithIndifferentAccess: use composition instead of inheritance
- delegate: use class_eval with string instead of define_method closures

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BgHHiteWiwPMhMcDAwpo1G

* require `set` by default.

* Add Integer#size, defined?(@@cvar), File constants, socket/wait stubs

- Integer#size: returns byte size of machine representation (needed by concurrent-ruby)
- defined?(@@class_var): full bytecode pipeline (DefinedCvar opcode 88) for class variable defined? checks (needed by CGI/i18n)
- File::BINARY, RDONLY, WRONLY, etc.: file mode constants (needed by CGI)
- socket.rb stub: minimal Socket/TCPSocket class definitions for Bundler
- wait.rb stub: minimal IO#wait_readable/wait_writable for io/wait
- ActiveRecord benchmark script (test_ar_bench.rb)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01BgHHiteWiwPMhMcDAwpo1G

* Fix Fiddle compatibility for ruby-bench benchmarks

- Add Fiddle::TYPE_* top-level constants by mirroring Fiddle::Types::*,
  matching CRuby's fiddle gem behaviour (fixes NameError on TYPE_INT)
- Define Kernel#no_warning_require at startup to prevent CRuby 4.0's
  bundled_gems.rb from patching require with unnecessary warnings

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Object#itself and Object#frozen? to startup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Random#rand instance method with tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add String#getbyte and String#setbyte methods

Implements getbyte/setbyte for String to support ruby-bench ruby-xor benchmark.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix use-after-return bug in generate_lambda causing GC panic

generate_lambda called move_frame_to_heap() but discarded the returned
heap Lfp, using the original stack Lfp instead. After the enclosing
function returned and the stack was reused, GC would try to mark through
the stale stack pointer and panic on corrupted object headers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Allow duplicate `_`-prefixed block parameters in bytecode generation

Ruby permits `_` (and `_`-prefixed names) to appear multiple times in
block parameter lists (e.g. `|(host, location, _, _)|`).  The previous
code asserted uniqueness and panicked on the second occurrence.

Now duplicate `_`-prefixed params each get their own register slot to
consume the argument, while the name resolves to the first occurrence,
matching CRuby behaviour.  Fixes rack benchmark panic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Use <=> instead of < / > in min_by and max_by implementations

The Ruby implementations of Enumerable#min_by, Enumerable#max_by, and
Hash#min_by used < and > for comparisons, which fails when the block
yields Array values (Array does not include Comparable).  Using <=> works
with any type that defines it, matching CRuby behaviour.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix private method_missing dispatch and args_to_vec argument order

method_missing is conventionally defined as private in Ruby, but the VM
must still dispatch to it when a method is not found. Previously monoruby
rejected the call due to visibility checks. Also fix args_to_vec which
returned arguments in reversed order due to the downward-growing stack.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: swap mssql/server for azure-sql-edge on ARM64 hosts

The Azure Service Bus emulator depends on SQL Server, which has no ARM64
image. On Apple Silicon (and other ARM64 hosts), the mssql/server:2022-latest
container fails under QEMU with jemalloc address mapping errors.

Use Aspire's BeforeStartEvent to intercept the container model and swap the
image to azure-sql-edge:latest, which has native ARM64 support. The Service
Bus emulator works with either SQL engine.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
